title: carex pilulifera var. bastardiana
intro: Carex pilulifera var. bastardiana, also known as Bastard Sedge, is a variety of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, Bastard Sedge has been used for its medicinal properties, which include being anti-inflammatory and expectorant, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Bastard Sedge can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, where its medicinal properties are often combined with other herbs to create a soothing and healing remedy.
